title: JWT Debugger & Decoder | Free Online Tool
description: Decode, verify, and validate JSON Web Tokens (JWT) instantly in your browser. View header, payload, and signature. Secure, offline-capable, and free.

schemas.name: JWT Debugger & Decoder
schemas.description: Decode, verify, and validate JSON Web Tokens (JWT) instantly in your browser. View header, payload, and signature.

acceptedAnswer.text: A JWT debugger decodes and inspects JSON Web Tokens without needing the secret key. It splits the token into its three parts (header, payload, signature), decodes the Base64URL-encoded header and payload, and displays the claims in readable JSON format. Use it to inspect token contents, check expiration times, and debug authentication issues.

mainEntity.name: Is it safe to paste my JWT into an online debugger?

acceptedAnswer.text: Our JWT debugger runs entirely in your browser — your token is never sent to any server. However, you should never paste production tokens containing sensitive data into tools that transmit data to a server. Always verify the tool is client-side only.

mainEntity.name: What are the three parts of a JWT?

acceptedAnswer.text: A JWT has three Base64URL-encoded parts separated by dots: the Header (algorithm and token type), the Payload (claims like sub, iat, exp), and the Signature (cryptographic verification). Our debugger decodes all three parts for inspection.
